danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile craft emerging from center vanishing point or screen edges in swirling patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring destruction to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Two generator segments with visible laser beam between them
danger_signals.meaning: Destructive environmental hazard; contact causes ship destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Rocks flying straight outward from screen center at regular intervals
danger_signals.meaning: Unavoidable obstacle; cannot be destroyed, but collision damage implied

danger_signals.visual_form: Group of three satellites materializing in small gyrating circles near player position
danger_signals.meaning: Concentrated threat formation with offensive capability

danger_signals.visual_form: Planet-specific boss ship appears at stage completion
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated difficulty; major threat to progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Screen transitions to bonus round after planet arrival cutscene
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safe zone where player cannot be destroyed; enemy ships present for points only

safety_signals.visual_form: Middle satellite appears as sun-like object (distinct visual variant)
safety_signals.meaning: Beneficial pickup; destroying grants enhanced weapon capability

safety_signals.signal: Stage 1 and every 10th stage enemy behavior
safety_signals.visual_form: Enemies deploy without firing during entrance sequence
safety_signals.meaning: Brief window of reduced danger during initial enemy appearance

safety_signals.visual_form: Satellite drops or special pickup from destroyed enemies

faction_indicators.faction: Player faction (Space Defense Force Gyruss / Rudolf's resistance)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Single player-controlled ship on perimeter circumference
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ship represents lone hero opposing tyranny; positioned at edge of circular play field, shooting inward toward center

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Multiple enemy spaceship types with varying behaviors; boss encounters per planet
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard enemy ships vs. planet-specific boss variants; satellite formations represent organized military presence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemies emerge from center vanishing point (authoritarian power emanating from solar system core); occupy spatial center of play field

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Game progression through solar system stages
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential planetary travel: 2 warps to Neptune → Uranus → Saturn → Jupiter → Mars → Earth (arcade); NES adds Pluto, Venus, Mercury, Sun for 39 total stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy difficulty increases with each planet; boss encounters trigger at planet arrivals; bonus stages provide reprieve after major threats

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Tyrannical Federation rule (introductory story state - FDS/JP version)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Text scroll intro showing oppressive labor conditions; military oversight imagery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player narrative begins in subjugation; rebellion formation (Gyruss) occurs after 10-year exile sentence completion

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Evil leader's invasion campaign (story escalation point)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Leader Domesu directs invasion command; visual shift to hostile space encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become more aggressive; player forced into direct confrontation across solar system

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Brief text: 'THE UNIVERSE IS AT PEACE' after final boss defeat; ship returns to sun and back to Earth
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game resets for another cycle; NES version minimizes narrative resolution compared to FDS epilogue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extended ending sequence with ship landing at base; full staff roll displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rudolf launches successful revolution on Earth, defeats father Domesu, ushers new era

environmental_storytelling.example: Player ship confined to circular perimeter while enemies emerge from center vanishing point; visual metaphor for single hero confronting centralized power structure
Journey from outer planets (Neptune, Uranus) toward inner system (Earth) mirrors approach to power center; each planet defeat represents reclaiming territory from oppressive regime
Distinct visual transition to bonus round after each planet; enemies present but non-threatening; represents moments of military rest/regime vulnerability

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy formation patterns as military organization
environmental_storytelling.example: Satellites deploy in rigid groups of three; swirling patterns suggest coordinated Federation forces; destruction of leader satellite (sun-like center) grants weapon upgrade
All enemy emergence and player shots converge at screen center; visual reinforcement that conflict originates from centralized authority

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Swirling pattern movement from center or edges; completes deployment pattern then hovers near center screen
Occasional offensive fly-outs toward player perimeter with projectile fire; gradual retreat if not destroyed
Deploy without firing; reduced initial threat allowing safe weapon/power gathering
Gyrate in small circles after deployment; fire at player continuously; center satellite (when basic weapon equipped) appears as distinctive sun-like object
Enhanced attack patterns; greater resilience; represents escalated military opposition specific to each conquered world
Flies straight outward from center; emits destructive beam between two segments; persists until either segment destroyed
Fly straight outward at regular intervals; indestructible; provide point bonus on hit (no tactical threat despite collision hazard)

notes: Gyruss employs circular one-point perspective to communicate political hierarchy: player as lone resistance fighter occupies defensive perimeter position while Federation forces emerge from centralized vanishing point. NES version significantly reduces narrative context (removes intro text explaining tyranny, eliminates epilogue showing post-war revolution) compared to FDS, weakening world-state communication. Bonus stages function as both mechanical reprieve and narrative respite from oppressive enemy presence. Enemy behavior variance (firing/non-firing on specific stages) creates punctuated difficulty rhythm implying military deployment cycles. Satellite formations and boss encounters escalate visual/behavioral threat intensity as player approaches solar system core (metaphorically approaching Domesu's seat of power).
